title: Psychotria podocephala
taxonomy: Psychotria podocephala is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The genus Psychotria is a group of tropical plants commonly known as wild coffee or joe pye weed, although they do not contain caffeine. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants with over 13,000 species worldwide, many of which are found in the tropics. This family is divided into several subfamilies, including the Psychotrieae, which contains the Psychotria genus. Psychotria podocephala is likely a member of the Psychotrieae subfamily within the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Psychotria podocephala, also known as P. podocephala, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of P. podocephala include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, P. podocephala is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of extracts and tinctures, to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
